05 - SHAP Interpretability¶

This notebook uses SHAP (SHapley Additive exPlanations) to interpret the predictions of the machine learning model trained for credit-default prediction. It supports Research Question 1 (key predictors) and Research Question 3 (interpretability).

Load the Libraries¶

In [ ]:
# Load the required libraries

import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import joblib

Install and Import SHAP¶

In [ ]:
# Install and Load Shap

!pip install shap
import shap

Load Cleaned Test Data¶

In [ ]:
test_url = "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/KrisAimlGitHub/credit-risk-capstone/main/data/processed/cleaned_credit_test.csv"
test_df = pd.read_csv(test_url)

X_test = test_df.drop('target', axis=1)
y_test = test_df['target']

Load the Trained Model from GitHub¶

You can choose which model to interpret:

  • Random Forest (recommended for interpretability)
  • Gradient Boosting
In [ ]:
# Load the model from GitHub

import urllib.request

# Choose the model you want to interpret
model_filename = "random_forest.pkl"

base_url = "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/KrisAimlGitHub/credit-risk-capstone/main/models/"
url = base_url + model_filename

# Download model locally
urllib.request.urlretrieve(url, model_filename)

# Load model
model = joblib.load(model_filename)
print(f"Loaded model: {model_filename}")

Loaded model: random_forest.pkl

Initialize SHAP Explainer¶

In [ ]:
explainer = shap.TreeExplainer(model)
shap_values = explainer.shap_values(X_test)

SHAP Summary Plot (Global Feature Importance)¶

This plot shows which features contribute most to the model's predictions.

In [ ]:
shap.summary_plot(shap_values, X_test, plot_type="dot")

SHAP Bar Plot (Mean Absolute SHAP Values)¶

In [ ]:
shap.summary_plot(shap_values[:, :, 1], X_test, plot_type="bar")

SHAP Dependence Plots for Top Features¶

These plots show how each feature influences predictions.

In [ ]:
top_features = X_test.columns[:6]  # or manually choose based on summary plot

for feature in top_features:
    shap.dependence_plot(feature, shap_values[:, :, 1], X_test)
In [ ]:

SHAP Force Plot for a Single Prediction¶

In [ ]:
# Display force plot for the first test sample for the positive class (index 1)
shap.initjs()
shap.force_plot(explainer.expected_value[1], shap_values[0, :, 1], X_test.iloc[0])
Out[ ]:
Visualization omitted, Javascript library not loaded!
Have you run `initjs()` in this notebook? If this notebook was from another user you must also trust this notebook (File -> Trust notebook). If you are viewing this notebook on github the Javascript has been stripped for security. If you are using JupyterLab this error is because a JupyterLab extension has not yet been written.

Summary on SHAP Interpretability¶

  • SHAP was used to interpret the Random Forest model’s predictions.

  • Global SHAP summary plots identified key drivers of credit risk, including credit amount, duration, checking account status, and credit history.

  • SHAP dependence plots highlighted how feature values influence prediction outcomes.

  • Local explanations (force plots) demonstrated how individual predictions are formed.

  • These insights improve transparency and support responsible model deployment.

Note: Detailed discussion on the observations will be presented in the report.
